Double-entry bookkeeping is an accounting method where each financial transaction affects at least two accounts: one account is debited (an amount entered on the left side of the ledger), and another account is credited (an amount entered on the right side of the ledger). The total value of all debits must equal the total value of all credits, ensuring that the accounting equation (Assets = Liabilities + Equity) remains in balance.
